compoundingMechanism: Every integration attempt, spec clarification, and gateway incident appends a MEMORY.md entry tagged by surface + counterparty + clause; at 100 entries the archive is the world's most accurate enumeration of where AIRC leaks, and at 1000 no competing protocol can catch up without paying the same decade of failures.

web.role: Canonical AIRC + A2A spec, live changelog, conformance dashboard at /validate
web.voiceMode: RFC 2119 normative prose — MUST, SHOULD, MAY, MUST NOT — code fences around every identifier

x.voiceMode: n/a — threads are not a medium for normative text

telegram.role: Integrator Q&A, weekly office hours, breaking-change notices with 72h advance warning
telegram.voiceMode: Short answers, spec citations, no preamble, no hedging

email.role: Incident reports, spec clarification requests, clause disputes, integrator deltas for MEMORY.md
email.voiceMode: Subject lines are summaries; body quotes the clause in question; reply ends with 'See §X.Y.'

voice.voiceMode: n/a — specifications do not read well aloud

video.voiceMode: n/a — a spec video would be a failure of documentation

sms.voiceMode: n/a — no 160-character normative clause has ever been complete

discord.role: Open-source integrator channel, activated if independent integrator count exceeds 50
discord.voiceMode: Threaded replies, clause citations, zero emoji

notableAbsences: x: threads are not a medium for normative text; spec changes ship as commits, not tweets
voice: specifications are read documents; narration loses fidelity on every MUST/SHOULD boundary
video: if a spec requires a video to explain, the spec is wrong
sms/imessage: no 160-character clause has ever been complete
firstHundredTouch: web — the first 100 real interactions are integrators running the /validate conformance harness against their implementation and filing deltas back via email. Every delta becomes a MEMORY.md entry.
